summary: Iteration 6 - Validated refactor of Services.js (1022→335 LOC) and CrearReporte.js (707→289 LOC) + new Web Push Notifications feature (VAPID). Backend: 16/16 pytest tests passed. Frontend: Services modal shows all 6 sections (Cliente, Ubicación, Facturación, Asignación, Servicios, Recomendaciones); CrearReporte loads with service selector and search; PushNotificationsCard renders on /configuracion with expected 'Permiso bloqueado' state in headless. Service worker contains push + notificationclick handlers and is served at /service-worker.js.

issues: Pre-existing React DOM warning '<span> cannot be a child of <option>/<select>' (carried over from iteration 5, no functional impact)

critical_code_review_comments: notifications.py opens a new AsyncIOMotorClient on every request via get_db() - minor efficiency concern; consider reusing the shared client from server.py (low priority).

send_push_to_user in push_service.py is async but calls pywebpush synchronously (blocking I/O). For high-volume push fan-out, wrap with asyncio.to_thread() or use httpx-based webpush. Currently fine for typical chat-message volume.
VAPID_PRIVATE_KEY committed in backend/.env is acceptable for preview environment, but should be rotated for production deployment.
Subscribe upsert is keyed only on endpoint (correct for unique device-endpoint pairs). Good idempotency.
seed_data_creation: None for this iteration. Test push subscription with endpoint TEST-ITER6-... is auto-cleaned by /api/notifications/test (dead endpoint -> removed) and explicit unsubscribe in fixture teardown.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Refactor is stable - all backend endpoints used by Services and CrearReporte continue to respond 200. Push notifications fully functional in backend (vapid-public-key, subscribe, status, test, unsubscribe). Frontend Configuracion shows PushNotificationsCard at the top with data-testid='push-notifications-card'. In headless Playwright the card correctly shows 'Permiso bloqueado'. Service worker at /service-worker.js contains both 'push' and 'notificationclick' event listeners. End-to-end push delivery (actual Notification API) cannot be validated in headless mode - requires real browser with user gesture.

backend_push_notifications: POST /api/notifications/test with fake FCM endpoint -> 200 with result {sent, failed, removed}; dead subs auto-removed
Auth required on subscribe/status/test -> 401/403 when no cookie
frontend: Login flow with admin credentials -> dashboard redirect
/services page: list renders; Nueva Orden modal opens with all 6 sections (Cliente, Ubicación, Facturación, Asignación, Servicios, Recomendaciones); search input functional
/reportes/crear page: service selector visible; search input functional; firma section present
/configuracion page: PushNotificationsCard renders at top with data-testid='push-notifications-card', title 'Notificaciones Push' visible, state 'Permiso bloqueado' shown (expected in headless mode)
/service-worker.js served with HTTP 200 and contains push + notificationclick handlers

rca_of_issues: No issues found in this iteration. Refactor preserved all existing behavior; new push notifications backend works as designed (including auto-cleanup of dead subscriptions returning 404/410).
